That's what the dream state was of this, is this is the escape hatch to add componentry that doesn't have native in our WYSIWYG builder, but then you can apply the WYSIWYG parts to it, and can drag it around on site.

And so, yes, but in our library display, I think toggling between style sheets would be a really slick way to say, look, this is how things can be, here's the style controls you have, here are the classes that can be introduced in this.
And if we think about that enough up front, that doesn't be, that's not difficult, right?

You do it once really, really well, you make sure all the components follow whatever guidelines, like in theory, not saying it's easy, but like it's not incredibly complicated to do that if you're thinking about that from the beginning versus trying to add something like that in halfway through.
Yeah.
If we were to focus on this, because I know it's a limited amount of people, I would...
I'd rather focus on this code component part of it, and as we progress, occasionally what's a really cool app example of using these things, but I don't think they have to run in parallel streams.
I think getting this part done is great, and then bridging together.
Okay, so maybe Liz, if you can help me think, be like, what, I guess what needs to happen now for us to get closer, right?
Is it something, is it like, does our team need to kind of sit down and do some thinking about this?
Are there other people we need to have a meeting with on your side, Colin, to like talk through some of the specifics?
Like, what would be the most helpful, like, next thing?

I think one of the first, I think a couple of the first meetings of actually talking about how we're going to approach this, I want to bring in the new PM lead who is owning the official part of code component and what we're calling DevLink.
She's very open to this world.
She's very excited about us building an example set and demo.
That a good adoption.
And maybe, maybe they're engineering lead, but honestly, this is primarily a DevRel thing.
So I think on our side, we're comfortable.
Yeah.
There's not like a crazy amount of engineering that kind of needs to happen outside of like writing the components.
Correct.
Unless there is capability features that we want to introduce on the import, because part of this, I think, was a very clever idea.
If we have a, we do have a Webflow CLI.

So if there is a way to have this, if it, if these components are stored in the repo that is tied to our package, then it would be really slick to be able to do any bash import Webflow CLI of the components you want to import.
Right.
And so making sure all that plays together is going to be where there is some engineering on our side to actually engage on.
Right.
That makes sense.
I think that.
In my mind, it's like there's quite a few steps before we get there, right, that we could do in the meantime.
Correct, correct.

But making sure they're informed is where I'd like to have it kick off once we are ready to start moving on this with them so everyone's aware of all the different moving parts because they might tell us to make that work on their package set, they need to be a month ahead of us.
Yeah, right.

Okay, I can at least, Liz, like sometime before the end of this week, just like, I don't know, probably just yell at Claude for 30 minutes about all the things that I'm thinking and then try to consolidate that down into like a some, like at least a document of questions to answer at the minimum.
And maybe some ideas and opinions and things and that can at least give us something to talk about the next time.

Yeah, we can get this on paper, reschedule this call, get a call scheduled with that PM and then ideally I'd want this all scoped so that by the time we actually kick off the sprint we all know what our tasks are so those are like those.
Things happening before that date.
So no block on that, but I'll follow up async for those pieces.
Yeah.
Great.
Right.
I guess that's it.
Cool.
Thanks.
Yeah.
Thanks very much, Colin.
Great to meet you guys.
Thank you.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[To scope the upcoming Code Components and AppGen initiative.](https://fathom.video/share/kLeNYFEAVeiFGPhn3TC7xzys75X4x6aL?tab=summary&timestamp=1373.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Strategic Pivot:** The initiative shifts from a marketing-focused "AppGen" showcase to a practical "Cloud" library of Code Components and example apps.](https://fathom.video/share/kLeNYFEAVeiFGPhn3TC7xzys75X4x6aL?tab=summary&timestamp=1565.0)
  - [**Core Goal:** Provide a library of pre-built Code Components (e.g., sliders, accordions) to fill gaps in Webflow's native library and lower the barrier to entry for new users.](https://fathom.video/share/kLeNYFEAVeiFGPhn3TC7xzys75X4x6aL?tab=summary&timestamp=1488.0)
  - [**Technical Constraint:** Webflow's live-rendering system ("Wooftle") limits standard frameworks like Tailwind. This necessitates a custom library of components modified to fit these rules.](https://fathom.video/share/kLeNYFEAVeiFGPhn3TC7xzys75X4x6aL?tab=summary&timestamp=2908.0)
  - [**Phased Approach:** Prioritize the Code Components library first, then build example apps that combine these components with Webflow Cloud services (e.g., a Greenhouse careers page).](https://fathom.video/share/kLeNYFEAVeiFGPhn3TC7xzys75X4x6aL?tab=summary&timestamp=3115.0)

## Topics

### Strategic Pivot: From "AppGen" to "Cloud"

  - [The initiative's focus has shifted from a marketing-driven "AppGen" showcase to a practical "Cloud" library.](https://fathom.video/share/kLeNYFEAVeiFGPhn3TC7xzys75X4x6aL?tab=summary&timestamp=1565.0)
  - [**Rationale:** The original "fun inspiration" approach lacked clear ROI and failed to resonate with developers, who need useful, B2B-focused tools.](https://fathom.video/share/kLeNYFEAVeiFGPhn3TC7xzys75X4x6aL?tab=summary&timestamp=1565.0)
  - [**New Focus:** A library of pre-built Code Components and example apps that solve real-world problems, such as a middleware service for an ABM campaign.](https://fathom.video/share/kLeNYFEAVeiFGPhn3TC7xzys75X4x6aL?tab=summary&timestamp=1713.0)

### Code Components: The Core Library

  - [**Goal:** A library of pre-built components (e.g., sliders, accordions) to fill gaps in Webflow's native library and lower the barrier to entry.](https://fathom.video/share/kLeNYFEAVeiFGPhn3TC7xzys75X4x6aL?tab=summary&timestamp=1488.0)
  - [**Technical Constraint:** Webflow's live-rendering system ("Wooftle") limits standard frameworks like Tailwind.](https://fathom.video/share/kLeNYFEAVeiFGPhn3TC7xzys75X4x6aL?tab=summary&timestamp=2908.0)
      - [**Impact:** This constraint prevents simply importing existing libraries like ShadCN, making a custom library essential.](https://fathom.video/share/kLeNYFEAVeiFGPhn3TC7xzys75X4x6aL?tab=summary&timestamp=2908.0)
  - [**Design Strategy:**](https://fathom.video/share/kLeNYFEAVeiFGPhn3TC7xzys75X4x6aL?tab=summary&timestamp=2356.0)
      - [Offer multiple styles for each component to provide variety without imposing a single design system.](https://fathom.video/share/kLeNYFEAVeiFGPhn3TC7xzys75X4x6aL?tab=summary&timestamp=2356.0)
      - [Prioritize adaptability: components should integrate with a user's existing site styles (colors, fonts) via a clear class-naming convention.](https://fathom.video/share/kLeNYFEAVeiFGPhn3TC7xzys75X4x6aL?tab=summary&timestamp=3035.0)
  - [**Distribution:**](https://fathom.video/share/kLeNYFEAVeiFGPhn3TC7xzys75X4x6aL?tab=summary&timestamp=2040.0)
      - [**Repo:** A GitHub repository for engineers to clone and fork.](https://fathom.video/share/kLeNYFEAVeiFGPhn3TC7xzys75X4x6aL?tab=summary&timestamp=2040.0)
      - [**UI:** A CMS-driven showcase page with live iframes and documentation.](https://fathom.video/share/kLeNYFEAVeiFGPhn3TC7xzys75X4x6aL?tab=summary&timestamp=2040.0)
      - [**CLI:** Future integration with the Webflow CLI for direct import.](https://fathom.video/share/kLeNYFEAVeiFGPhn3TC7xzys75X4x6aL?tab=summary&timestamp=3201.0)

### Example Apps: Building with Cloud

  - [**Goal:** A "cookbook" of \~100 example apps demonstrating practical uses for Webflow Cloud services.](https://fathom.video/share/kLeNYFEAVeiFGPhn3TC7xzys75X4x6aL?tab=summary&timestamp=2264.0)
  - [**Integration:** Apps will combine Code Components with backend services (e.g., Node, Astro).](https://fathom.video/share/kLeNYFEAVeiFGPhn3TC7xzys75X4x6aL?tab=summary&timestamp=1713.0)
      - [**Example:** A Greenhouse integration app that uses a custom Code Component slider to display job listings.](https://fathom.video/share/kLeNYFEAVeiFGPhn3TC7xzys75X4x6aL?tab=summary&timestamp=1958.0)
  - [**Showcase:** Apps will be displayed in live iframes on the showcase page.](https://fathom.video/share/kLeNYFEAVeiFGPhn3TC7xzys75X4x6aL?tab=summary&timestamp=2104.0)
      - [**Challenge:** Creating meaningful visual demos for non-visual apps (e.g., middleware).](https://fathom.video/share/kLeNYFEAVeiFGPhn3TC7xzys75X4x6aL?tab=summary&timestamp=2250.0)

### Admin & Logistics

  - [**API Access:** Liz requested updated API access (CMS + assets write) to automate image uploads for integration pages, unblocking a manual workflow.](https://fathom.video/share/kLeNYFEAVeiFGPhn3TC7xzys75X4x6aL?tab=summary&timestamp=388.0)
  - [**Meeting Time:** The recurring sync time conflicts with Colin's schedule; Liz will coordinate a new time.](https://fathom.video/share/kLeNYFEAVeiFGPhn3TC7xzys75X4x6aL?tab=summary&timestamp=1339.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Kirkland:** Draft a document outlining questions and ideas for the Code Components library by EOW.](https://fathom.video/share/kLeNYFEAVeiFGPhn3TC7xzys75X4x6aL?tab=summary&timestamp=3261.0)
  - [**Liz:**](https://fathom.video/share/kLeNYFEAVeiFGPhn3TC7xzys75X4x6aL?tab=summary&timestamp=3288.0)
      - [Reschedule the weekly sync to a time that works for Colin.](https://fathom.video/share/kLeNYFEAVeiFGPhn3TC7xzys75X4x6aL?tab=summary&timestamp=1350.0)
      - [Schedule a follow-up meeting with the new PM lead for Code Components/DevLink.](https://fathom.video/share/kLeNYFEAVeiFGPhn3TC7xzys75X4x6aL?tab=summary&timestamp=3160.0)
  - [**Zach:** Provide Liz with the updated API token (CMS + assets write permissions) today.](https://fathom.video/share/kLeNYFEAVeiFGPhn3TC7xzys75X4x6aL?tab=summary&timestamp=602.0)

action_items.description: Post channel poll to reschedule weekly sync w/ Colin
Draft Code Components/Cloud scope doc; share w/ Liz + Colin
Schedule scoping mtg w/ Code Components/DevLink PM + Colin
